summary: A stellar audience tape from 9/16/90 with warm, clear sound and a second‑set that roars – standout versions of “He’s Gone”, “Standing on the Moon” and an epic “Morning Dew”.
review: The show is widely praised for its soaring second set, blistering jams (especially the He’s Gone jam and the post‑drums surge into Standing on the Moon → Miracle → Dew) and a thunderous Morning Dew that many call “monster”.  The audience recording is consistently described as warm, listenable and surprisingly detailed, with only occasional chatter or vocal mix quirks.  Overall the performance earns top marks for Jerry’s lead work, Phil’s bass brilliance and the whole band’s chemistry.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 20 reviews, most 4‑5 stars, uniformly praising the music and noting only minor recording quirks; strong weight on show quality pushes rating above the raw average.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Warm, well‑balanced audience tape with clear instrument separation. Reviewers note occasional audience chatter, a slightly low vocal mix for Jerry on some songs and occasional loudness from Vince, but overall very listenable.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The first set is solid but the second set ignites, featuring a long He’s Gone jam, a spectacular post‑drums sequence into Standing on the Moon → Miracle → Dew and several high‑energy jams that keep the momentum rolling.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for soaring lead guitar, magical solo moments (especially in He’s Gone and the post‑drums jam); vocal mix occasionally low but overall solid performance.
band_member_comments.Phil: Repeatedly highlighted as the show’s star – booming bass, inventive post‑drums work, use of an Aural Exciter that adds sparkle; many reviewers call his playing “all over it”.
band_member_comments.Bob: Only a few negative comments (some dislike for his “queer” style); otherwise not prominently mentioned.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (and later Vince) receive kudos for beautiful piano/chord work on Dew and creative jams with Phil; Hornsby’s contributions are noted as adding a 70‑s flavor.
band_member_comments.Drums: Billy & Mickey praised for a “sheeeaaaattt” post‑drums section, considered one of the best of the ’90s.
